title: Wireshark Export Objects — full HTTP-content list including the `/ned.exe` 7188 KB download
html:
Wireshark Export-Objects dialog filtered on HTTP — the smoking gun for the OSU lab exercise. Among the noise of normal Windows-update CTL/CRL chatter (disallowedcertstl.cab, pinrulesstl.cab, OCSP responses to GlobalSign / DigiCert / GeoTrust / Google PKI) sits one alarming row: packet 7085 from internal 192.168.157.129 serving an application/x-msdos-program of 7188 kB named ned.exe. Plus a stream of msedge.b.tlu.dl.delivery.mp.microsoft.com Chrome-extension transfers and msv-r061.video.microsoft.com MP4 fragments.

TL;DR

  • The /ned.exe 7.2 MB binary served by an internal lab host (192.168.157.129) is the planted-malware artifact in the network forensics exercise.
  • The surrounding traffic is legitimate Windows trust-store updates + OCSP (GlobalSign / DigiCert / GeoTrust / Google PKI) + Edge auto-updates + a streaming-video session — i.e., realistic noise to make the C2 needle hard to find.
  • Confirms this is the same FINAL_forreal.pcapng referenced in 2025 06 10 screenshot 2025 06 10 at 1 05 42 am f1abc915 and the two OCSP packet-detail screenshots from 1:09 pm.

wireshark · packet capture · network forensics · malware analysis lab · mobile malware · osu
